name: The Demon's Apple - Otome Game
short_description: The Demon's Apple is a Dark Fantasy otome game with mature content. Pomme, a herbalist as to make an alliance with a bloodthirsty demon she saved to survive in a world hostile to both. Play as Pomme who has to learn to live under the same roof as the demon who wants her blood and more.
detailed_description:
The Demon's Apple is a Dark Fantasy Otome game with mature content.

Pomme is the village herbalist and during one of her outings to pick up herbs used to create medecine she finds a badly wounded man and takes him home to try to save his life. She then discovers he is a demon, bloodthirsty creature, as he bites her to feed himself before escaping. 

But they meet again... And this time they will have to cooperate to survive in a world hostile to both humans and demons.

The game features:

  • Seven chapters of story (about 3 hours of gameplay) 

  • A cast of diverse characters

  • A slightly intimidating demon love interest

  • About 20 main CGs, more than doubled if we take into every CGs 

  • Mature content

  • Three endings

The characters:

reviews.review_text:
A slow burn romance with hints of spice to make it nice. Well written and gorgeous illustrations. 

[h3][u]Gameplay & Settings[/u][/h3]

You play as Pomme (name is changeable but such a great mc name), who is the village herbalist. Out collecting ingredients in the forest you come across a wounded demon who you foolishly help. Surrounded by dangers he shacks up with you much to your dismay. Demons need human mana to survive and that means you're on the menu…delightful! 

I highly enjoyed the story, it is very well written and the encounters between Styx and Pomme are a slow burning romance with some spice mixed in. I was blushing a few times! 

🔹One play-though took me about an hour and a bit (will depend on your reading speed). There are three endings in total to achieve. Sadly no achievements for this one at this time. 

🌶️ Spice Level - High. Definitely spicy. 

Settings & extras include; display options, skip text options, language, text speed, auto-forward time speed, and audio controls. There are plenty of save slots and you can hide the hud to enjoy the artwork or take screen captures. The text is very easy to read with a grey font on a dark background. 

Extras include a gallery you can unlock as you play. 

[h3][u]The Visuals & Audio[/u][/h3]

I really enjoy the artwork in this game. The character art is vibrant and well illustrated and I really have to point out how well the backgrounds are done in this novel. It is so refreshing to see attention given to them and not just the sprites. There are also particle effects in some backgrounds to give the look of light streaming into the environments (adds a magical feel). 

The novel has no voice acting or ambient sounds but the soundtrack is enjoyable. Instrumental and gives a nice feel to the story.

🦋 For lovers of cg’s this one is jam-packed with gorgeous special moments and spicy ones too. 

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3689131433
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3689131179

[h3][u]Overall Experience[/u][/h3]

If you're in the mood for a spicier novel with some great cg’s and a slow burning relationship then this one is for you. I always enjoy when the spice is done right. Not just in-your-face and graphic. There are some tender moments, some funny ones and of course some sexy ones.

I will be following the developer [i]Kakera[/i] and looking forward to their future novel coming out called [i]Mask-Beyond Lies[/i] as I highly enjoyed this novel.💗

[hr][/hr]
[quote]Please follow our curator page, [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/4680120/]Otome Lovers[/url], if you'd like to see more reviews like this one! [/quote]

Nice soundtrack, nice story, nice artwork, i would give it a 7/10 my only suggestion is that the writing could be a bit more mature, and flesh out the story more. The MC's thinking process makes her out to be a naive and a doormat (I don't mind doormat MC's but she was a bit too much lol). 

I see the potential, the world-building is interesting, but maybe needs more work in terms of writing.
-----------
SPOILERS AHEAD:

I kept waiting for her to get mad at Styx after he used and left her when they met again, but she was too welcoming without even acting slightly cold or hurt or anything, it put me off a little bit. Also her dad doesn't act like a dad at all, because let's be real what father would be fine with his daughter to be left alone with a man and a demon at that back at the inn, maybe I'm too arab for this cuz our dads would break the door if we closed it after that encounter lmao.

That said though, i don't regret buying or playing it. Waiting for your other work Kakera.

Bride Styx! As a rabid fan of indie otome games and the romantasy genre, I have to say I enjoyed this short romp of a visual novel. And in a Bone, Bride, Banish husbando ranking? I Bride Styx! Why? He's HOT, OK? Also he cooks. HE COOKS! And he's protective. And when he loves, he loves hard. What's not to like? Ok yes he is bitey. I'll give you that. But he's precious, ok? Tee hee. Also I always appreciate being able to play in the female perspective in a story like this because it makes an impact on what the MC fears and values and how the built world treats her. We would have lost that impact if there was no established Main Character with her own identity. I got the Good Ending but I'll be back soon to 100% the rest of them.  *I was invited to play this game. This is my honest opinion.

I’m new to otome games, and I love how the video game format can tell stories and draw us in in different ways. I really enjoyed playing this game! 
It took me a little while to get into it, but once I did, I found it pretty engaging—I wanted to see what happened next and learn more about the world and the characters! The artwork is beautiful, and the scenes are well-crafted. The characters are endearing.
I was just left wanting a little more—I wanted to know more, there are still unanswered questions, and I really want to see more!
I got a really good ending :) 
Can’t wait to see the next games!

Not awful, but really not great. I can't recommend this game, but it had potential, so I have some feedback.

The English translation falls quite flat. There are just some weird bits that aren't polished in the dialogue. I can't speak for the original script, but based on the game as a whole, both leads feel... just devoid of all personality. That's one thing for a MC that players might just self-insert with, but the love interest should probably have a bit more substance. He was cute enough but totally boring, which ruins it.

For a game advertised as having adult content, it was extremely tame, basically just lackluster. The only actual nudity was the MC's (the girl you play as, to be very clear) nipples, lol. Just really not what I was expecting or hoping for in that regard. Felt a little shortchanged as I was mainly buying this for the sake of supporting an up-and-coming NSFW otome game, which is a somewhat underserved niche. 

As for positives of this game, I liked the art style and some concepts. The plot was soo meh by the end- I was expecting way more to actually be addressed and fleshed out, but it just wasn't. Felt like a lot of exposition about how MC is seemingly a witch, allusions to her mother, her wanting to help the demons, all for it to kind of be brushed under the rug. As I did play through the whole thing and it had some good moments and decent atmosphere, I won't say it's awful. but it was for sure underwhelming and somewhat disappointing.
